name: Current Affairs in TN History/Culture/Heritage (7): Tamil Nadu's 2026 National Para Athletics Performance

sourceRefs.label: outlookindia.com - National Para Athletics Championships 2026 medal tally (Tamil Nadu finished 2nd)

researchNotes: Seventh and final TN-Culture-unit current-affairs topic of this 7th lap, completing Part A's 7th full rotation. A sports-achievement angle, providing welcome variety after several consecutive awards/heritage-institution topics in this unit. Expansion pass (Batch 20): added context on what Para Athletics classification means and its inclusive-sports-policy significance.

reading.intro: Covers Tamil Nadu's performance at the National Para Athletics Championships 2026.

qa.question: Where did Tamil Nadu finish in the National Para Athletics Championships 2026 medal tally, and with how many total medals?
qa.answer: 2nd place, with 51 total medals (14 gold, 20 silver, 17 bronze).

qa.question: Which state topped the National Para Athletics Championships 2026 medal tally?
Which state finished 3rd in the National Para Athletics Championships 2026?
What financial incentive has the Tamil Nadu government separately announced for athletes?
qa.answer: Chief Minister M.K. Stalin announced a purse of ₹3 crore for Tamil Nadu sportspersons who win GOLD at the Olympics.

qa.question: What is Para Athletics, and why do national championships in this category matter for inclusive sports policy?
qa.answer: Athletics events specifically organised for athletes with physical/sensory/intellectual disabilities, following classification systems that ensure fair competition among athletes with similar impairment levels. Strong state performance and government investment (like TN's medal haul and Olympic incentive) signal growing institutional support for disability sports, not just able-bodied athletics.
